forecast-period.text: A series of low pressure systems passing over waters to the south will extend cold fronts that will cross over SA through to the end of the week. A high pressure system is then forecast to move into the Bight from late Friday and extend a ridge across the state over the weekend, as it slowly moves southeastwards towards Tasmania.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots in the afternoon. Winds turning southwesterly in the evening.
3 metres.
Westerly 4 to 6 metres, tending southwesterly 5 to 7 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds decreasing to about 10 knots in the late afternoon.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 4 to 5 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly below 10 knots tending east to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots in the afternoon.
2.5 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning southwesterly 15 to 25 knots before dawn then decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the middle of the day.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
West to southwesterly 2 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 to 2 metres by early evening.
Cloudy.
South to southwesterly about 10 knots tending southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the afternoon.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 30 to 35 knots.
3 metres.
Westerly 4 to 5 metres, increasing to 4 to 7 metres around midday, then tending southwesterly 6 to 7 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon. Possible small hail in the afternoon and evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Friday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots early in the morning. Winds decreasing to 15 to 25 knots in the morning.
2.5 to 4 metres, decreasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 5 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Cloudy.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 35 knots.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 4 metres, increasing to 4 to 6 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Salt Creek. Possible small hail south of Salt Creek in the late afternoon and evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Friday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: West to southwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots early in the morning. Winds becoming southwesterly 15 to 25 knots in the middle of the day.
3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 5 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 5 metres.
Cloudy.
